Homework 7

Frank Marino

March 22, 2026

Canva and Tableau is not used for this assignment. There is also no presentation for this assignment.

Set-up

Homework 7 follows a tutorial for mapping spatio-temporal data using the storms data from the dplyr package. Click here to access the full tutorial.

Load Libraries

# Load any R Packages you may need
rm(list = ls())
library(mosaic)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(sf)
library(mapview)

Load Data

You’ll want to change the names of the Hurricanes in Line 42 from Sandy, Katrina, and Jerry to 3 other hurricane names. Below is a code chunk you can run to view a list of the summary statistics for wind speed with the names of all the storms in this data set.

From this list, choose THREE Names to edit in the following code chunk in Line 42:

# Load Data
storms_data <- dplyr::storms
storms_data <- filter(storms_data, name == "Frances" | name == "Bret" | name == "Hortense")
storms_data <- filter(storms_data, status == "tropical depression" | status == "tropical storm" | status == "hurricane")

DO NOT EDIT THIS CODE CHUNK

Exercise 1

(a)

Create a base R plot that maps the tracks of the storms using longitude and latitude, while color coding by storm status.

storms.sf <- storms_data %>% 
    st_as_sf(coords = c("long", "lat"), crs = 4326) 
class(storms.sf)
## [1] "sf"         "tbl_df"     "tbl"        "data.frame"
storms.sf %>% select(name, year, geometry)
## Simple feature collection with 442 features and 2 fields
## Geometry type: POINT
## Dimension:     XY
## Bounding box:  xmin: -103 ymin: 9.2 xmax: -19 ymax: 51.2
## Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84
## # A tibble: 442 × 3
##    name     year     geometry
##    <chr>   <dbl>  <POINT [°]>
##  1 Frances  1976 (-37.5 12.5)
##  2 Frances  1976 (-45.3 14.7)
##  3 Frances  1976 (-49.6 16.6)
##  4 Frances  1976 (-50.5 17.5)
##  5 Frances  1976 (-51.6 18.3)
##  6 Frances  1976 (-54.2 20.2)
##  7 Frances  1976   (-54.9 21)
##  8 Frances  1976 (-55.2 23.1)
##  9 Frances  1976 (-54.9 25.3)
## 10 Frances  1976 (-54.3 26.3)
## # ℹ 432 more rows
storms.sf <- storms.sf %>% 
    mutate(time = as.POSIXct(paste(paste(year,month,day, sep = "-"), 
                                   paste(hour, ":00", sep = "")))) %>% 
    select(-month, -day, -hour)
storms.sf %>% select(name, time, geometry)
## Simple feature collection with 442 features and 2 fields
## Geometry type: POINT
## Dimension:     XY
## Bounding box:  xmin: -103 ymin: 9.2 xmax: -19 ymax: 51.2
## Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84
## # A tibble: 442 × 3
##    name    time                    geometry
##    <chr>   <dttm>               <POINT [°]>
##  1 Frances 1976-08-27 12:00:00 (-37.5 12.5)
##  2 Frances 1976-08-28 18:00:00 (-45.3 14.7)
##  3 Frances 1976-08-29 12:00:00 (-49.6 16.6)
##  4 Frances 1976-08-29 18:00:00 (-50.5 17.5)
##  5 Frances 1976-08-30 00:00:00 (-51.6 18.3)
##  6 Frances 1976-08-30 18:00:00 (-54.2 20.2)
##  7 Frances 1976-08-31 00:00:00   (-54.9 21)
##  8 Frances 1976-08-31 12:00:00 (-55.2 23.1)
##  9 Frances 1976-09-01 00:00:00 (-54.9 25.3)
## 10 Frances 1976-09-01 06:00:00 (-54.3 26.3)
## # ℹ 432 more rows
status_colors <- c(
  "hurricane"           = "#D85A30",
  "tropical storm"      = "#1D9E75",
  "tropical depression" = "#4488DD"
)

# Map each point's status to the correct color
point_colors <- status_colors[as.character(storms.sf$status)]

plot(st_geometry(storms.sf), 
     cex     = .5, 
     pch     = 19, 
     axes    = TRUE, 
     graticule = TRUE, 
     col     = point_colors)

legend("topleft", 
       legend = names(status_colors), 
       col    = status_colors, 
       pch    = 19,
       bty    = "n")

(b)

Create a plot using ggplot() that maps the tracks of the storms using longitude and latitude, while color coding by storm name.

(storms.nest <- storms.sf %>% group_by(name, year) %>% nest)
## # A tibble: 17 × 3
## # Groups:   name, year [17]
##    name      year data         
##    <chr>    <dbl> <list>       
##  1 Frances   1976 <sf [12 × 8]>
##  2 Frances   1980 <sf [60 × 8]>
##  3 Bret      1981 <sf [5 × 8]> 
##  4 Hortense  1984 <sf [33 × 8]>
##  5 Frances   1986 <sf [13 × 8]>
##  6 Bret      1987 <sf [26 × 8]>
##  7 Hortense  1990 <sf [26 × 8]>
##  8 Frances   1992 <sf [16 × 8]>
##  9 Bret      1993 <sf [33 × 8]>
## 10 Hortense  1996 <sf [50 × 8]>
## 11 Frances   1998 <sf [21 × 8]>
## 12 Bret      1999 <sf [26 × 8]>
## 13 Frances   2004 <sf [67 × 8]>
## 14 Bret      2005 <sf [7 × 8]> 
## 15 Bret      2011 <sf [19 × 8]>
## 16 Bret      2017 <sf [5 × 8]> 
## 17 Bret      2023 <sf [23 × 8]>
to_line <- function(tr) st_cast(st_combine(tr), "LINESTRING") %>% .[[1]] 
(tracks <- storms.nest %>% pull(data) %>% map(to_line) %>% st_sfc(crs = 4326))
## Geometry set for 17 features 
## Geometry type: LINESTRING
## Dimension:     XY
## Bounding box:  xmin: -103 ymin: 9.2 xmax: -19 ymax: 51.2
## Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84
## First 5 geometries:
## LINESTRING (-37.5 12.5, -45.3 14.7, -49.6 16.6,...
## LINESTRING (-19 12.5, -20 12.5, -21 12.6, -21.8...
## LINESTRING (-71 36, -72.5 36, -73.8 36.2, -74.7...
## LINESTRING (-60.1 28.5, -60.3 28.7, -60.4 28.9,...
## LINESTRING (-62.8 22.8, -62.9 23.5, -62.9 23.9,...
(storms.tr <- storms.nest %>% select(-data) %>% st_sf(geometry = tracks))
## Simple feature collection with 17 features and 2 fields
## Geometry type: LINESTRING
## Dimension:     XY
## Bounding box:  xmin: -103 ymin: 9.2 xmax: -19 ymax: 51.2
## Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84
## # A tibble: 17 × 3
## # Groups:   name, year [17]
##    name      year                                                       geometry
##    <chr>    <dbl>                                               <LINESTRING [°]>
##  1 Frances   1976 (-37.5 12.5, -45.3 14.7, -49.6 16.6, -50.5 17.5, -51.6 18.3, …
##  2 Frances   1980 (-19 12.5, -20 12.5, -21 12.6, -21.8 12.7, -22.5 12.8, -23.2 …
##  3 Bret      1981           (-71 36, -72.5 36, -73.8 36.2, -74.7 36.6, -76 37.4)
##  4 Hortense  1984 (-60.1 28.5, -60.3 28.7, -60.4 28.9, -60.6 29.3, -60.8 29.7, …
##  5 Frances   1986 (-62.8 22.8, -62.9 23.5, -62.9 23.9, -62.8 24.4, -62.7 24.8, …
##  6 Bret      1987 (-20 14.8, -22 14.9, -24 15, -26 15.1, -27.9 15.2, -29.5 15.3…
##  7 Hortense  1990 (-35 12.5, -36.1 12.9, -37.3 13.3, -38.5 13.7, -39.4 13.9, -4…
##  8 Frances   1992 (-61.4 27.7, -61.3 28.3, -61.1 28.7, -60.6 29, -59.9 29.6, -5…
##  9 Bret      1993 (-40.3 10.4, -41.7 10.4, -43.4 10.4, -45.3 10.3, -47.3 10.3, …
## 10 Hortense  1996 (-41 14.9, -42.7 14.9, -44.1 14.8, -45.4 14.7, -46.6 14.6, -4…
## 11 Frances   1998 (-94.5 25.5, -94.4 25.3, -94.5 25, -94.7 24.6, -95.5 24.2, -9…
## 12 Bret      1999 (-94.4 19.5, -94.5 19.5, -94.6 19.6, -94.6 19.7, -94.7 19.8, …
## 13 Frances   2004 (-35.2 11.1, -36.8 11.2, -38.3 11.3, -39.8 11.5, -41.5 11.9, …
## 14 Bret      2005 (-95.4 19.7, -95.7 19.9, -95.8 20, -96.4 20.4, -97.3 20.8, -9…
## 15 Bret      2011 (-78.2 27.8, -78.1 27.5, -78 27.1, -77.5 27.4, -77.1 27.8, -7…
## 16 Bret      2017     (-58.9 9.2, -60.5 9.7, -61.1 10.1, -62.1 10.4, -62.6 10.6)
## 17 Bret      2023 (-37.3 10.7, -39.1 10.9, -40.9 11.1, -42.7 11.3, -44.5 11.5, …
storms.tr %>% 
    ggplot(aes(color = name)) + geom_sf() + theme(legend.position="none")

(c)

Create a plot using ggplot() that maps the tracks of the storms using longitude and latitude, while color coding by year.

storms.tr %>% ggplot(aes(color = year)) + geom_sf()

(d)

Create a plot using mapview() that maps the tracks of the storms, while color coding by year.

library(mapview)
mapview(storms.tr, zcol = "year", legend = TRUE)

Exercise 2

(a)

Create a plot using ggplot() that maps the tracks of the storms using longitude and latitude, while color coding by wind.

to_lines <- function(tr) { 
    g = st_geometry(tr)
    hd = head(g, -1)
    tl = tail(g, -1)
    map2(hd, tl, function(x,y) st_combine(st_sfc(x, y, crs = 4326))) %>% 
        map(function(x) st_cast(x, "LINESTRING"))
}
trs <- storms.nest %>% 
    pull(data) %>% 
    map(to_lines) %>% 
    unlist(recursive = FALSE) %>% 
    do.call(c, .)
fn = function(x) head(x, -1) %>% as.data.frame %>% select(-geometry)
storms.nest <- storms.nest %>% mutate(data = map(data, fn))
storms.tr2 <- storms.nest %>% unnest %>% st_set_geometry(trs)
## Warning: `cols` is now required when using `unnest()`.
## ℹ Please use `cols = c(data)`.
nrow(storms.tr2)
## [1] 425
## [1] 9584
storms.tr2 %>% ggplot(aes(color = wind)) + geom_sf()

(b)

Create a plot that maps the tracks of the storms using mapview(), while color coding by wind.

mapview(storms.tr2, zcol = "wind", legend = TRUE)

Exercise 3

(a)

Now create a new subset to filter on the data to have time greater than January 1, 2010.

storms.tr3 <- storms.tr2 %>% 
    filter(time >= as.POSIXct("2010-01-01")) 
mapview(storms.tr3, zcol = "wind", lwd = 4)

(b)

Create a plot using ggplot() that maps the tracks of the storms using longitude and latitude, while color coding by wind.

plot(storms.tr3["wind"], type = 'p', cex = .2, pch = 16, axes = TRUE,
     graticule = TRUE)
plot(storms.tr3["wind"], type = 'l', add = TRUE)

(c)

Create a plot using mapview() that maps the tracks of the storms using, while color coding by wind.

mapview(storms.tr3, zcol = "wind", lwd = 4, legend = TRUE) +
  mapview(st_cast(storms.tr3, "POINT"), zcol = "wind", cex = 4)
## Warning in st_cast.sf(storms.tr3, "POINT"): repeating attributes for all
## sub-geometries for which they may not be constant

All done!

Knit the completed R Markdown file as a HTML document (click the “Knit” button at the top of the script editor window) and upload it to the submission portal on Canvas.
